title: rumex × mixtus
intro: Rumex × mixtus, also known as dock sorrel or dock mallow, is a hybrid plant that is a cross between the broad-leaved dock (Rumex obtusifolius) and the sorrel (Rumex acetosella). The medicinal properties of Rumex × mixtus include its astringent, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which are primarily due to its high concentration of tannins and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to treat digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate skin conditions, as well as its ability to act as a diuretic and an antiseptic. Rumex × mixtus can be prepared as a herbal tea, salve, or poultice, and its leaves can also be used fresh in salads or as a cooking green.
